title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 6 (Fragment 4)
text:
Old Niccolo cleared his throat. “Now look here,” he said. Then he stopped. This was Antonio’s business. He looked at Antonio.

Paolo and Tonino noticed that their father looked at their mother first. Elizabeth’s happy look seemed to reassure him a little. “Now, just who are you?” he said to the young man. “How did you meet my Rosa?”

“He was one of the contractors on the Old Bridge, Father,” said Rosa.

“And he has enormous natural talent, Antonio,” said Elizabeth, “and a beautiful singing voice.”

“All right, all right,” said Antonio. “Let the boy speak for himself, women.”

The young man swallowed, and helped the swallow down with a shake of his tie. His face was now very pale. “My name is Marco Andretti,” he said in a pleasant, if husky, voice. “I—I think you met my brother at the bridge, sir. I was on the other shift. That’s how I came to meet Rosa.” The way he smiled down at Rosa left everybody hoping that he would be fit to become a Montana.

“It’ll break their hearts if Father says no,” Lucia whispered to Paolo. Paolo nodded. He could see that.

Antonio was pulling his lip, which was a thing he did when his face could hold no more worry than it did already. “Yes,” he said. “I’ve met Mario Andretti, of course. A very respectable family.” He made that sound not altogether a good thing. “But I’m sure you’re aware, Signor Andretti, that ours is a special family. We have to be careful who we marry. First, what do you think of the Petrocchis?”

Marco’s pale face went fiery red. He answered with a violence which surprised the Montanas, “I hate their guts, Signor Montana!” He seemed so upset that Rosa pulled his arm down and patted it soothingly.

“Marco has personal family reasons, Father,” she said.

“Which I’d prefer not to go into,” Marco said.

“We—well, I’ll not press you for them,” Antonio said, and continued to pull his lip. “But, you see, our family must marry someone with at least some talent for magic. Have you any ability there, Signor Andretti?”

Marco Andretti seemed to relax at this. He smiled, and gently took Rosa’s hands off his sleeve. Then he sang. Elizabeth had been right about his voice. It was a golden tenor. Uncle Lorenzo was heard to rumble that he could not think what a voice like that was doing outside the Milan Opera.

“A golden tree there grows, a tree Whose golden branches bud with green. …”

sang Marco. As he sang, the tree came into being, rooted in the carpet between Rosa and Antonio, first as a faint gold shadow, then as a rattling metal shape, dazzling gold in the shafts of sunlight from the windows. The Montanas nodded their appreciation. The trunk and each branch, even the smallest twig, was indeed pure gold. But Marco sang on, and as he sang, the gold twigs put out buds, pale and fist-shaped at first, then bright and pointed. Instants later, the tree was in leaf. It was moving and rattling constantly to Marco’s singing. It put out pink and white flowers in clusters, which budded, expanded and dropped, as quickly as flames in a firework. The room was full of scent, then of petals fluttering like confetti. Marco still sang, and the tree still moved. Before the last petal had fallen, pointed green fruit was swelling where the flowers had been. The fruit grew brownish and swelled, and swelled and turned bulging and yellow, until the tree drooped under the weight of a heavy crop of big yellow pears.

“… With golden fruit for everyone,”

Marco concluded. He put up a hand, picked one of the pears and held it, rather diffidently, out to Antonio.

vocabulary.definition: Possession of the means or skill to do something.
vocabulary.example: Have you any ability there, Signor Andretti?

vocabulary.definition: Recognition and enjoyment of the good qualities of someone or something.
vocabulary.example: The Montanas nodded their appreciation.

vocabulary.definition: A small swelling on a plant that develops into a leaf, flower, or shoot.
vocabulary.example: Branches budded, expanded and dropped as flowers.

vocabulary.definition: A group of similar things or people positioned closely together.
vocabulary.example: Flowers appeared in clusters.

vocabulary.definition: A person or company that undertakes a contract to provide materials or labour to perform a service or do a job.
vocabulary.example: He was one of the contractors on the Old Bridge.

vocabulary.definition: Extremely bright, especially so as to blind the eyes temporarily or impress greatly.
vocabulary.example: Dazzling gold in the shafts of sunlight from the windows.

vocabulary.definition: In a manner showing modesty or lack of confidence.
vocabulary.example: He put up a hand and held a pear out diffidently.

vocabulary.definition: To bend or hang down limply, especially because of exhaustion or weight.
vocabulary.example: The tree drooped under the weight of the heavy crop of pears.

vocabulary.definition: Very large in size, quantity, or extent.
vocabulary.example: He has enormous natural talent.

vocabulary.definition: Having a very intense red colour, often indicating strong emotion such as anger or embarrassment.
vocabulary.example: His pale face went fiery red.

vocabulary.definition: Moving lightly and quickly; flapping or vibrating.
vocabulary.example: Petals fluttered like confetti.

vocabulary.definition: A highly valued or beautiful tenor singing voice described as golden for its quality.
vocabulary.example: It was a golden tenor.

vocabulary.definition: Having a slightly rough or hoarse sound, often in reference to a voice.
vocabulary.example: He said in a husky voice.

vocabulary.definition: An innate ability or aptitude for something.
vocabulary.example: He has enormous natural talent.

vocabulary.definition: Private or intimate matters related to one's family that influence behavior or decisions.
vocabulary.example: Marco has personal family reasons, which he preferred not to discuss.

vocabulary.definition: Giving a sense of happy satisfaction or enjoyment; friendly and considerate.
vocabulary.example: He said in a pleasant voice.

vocabulary.definition: To move or tense the lip, often indicating worry or anxiety.
vocabulary.example: Antonio was pulling his lip, which was a thing he did when worried.

vocabulary.definition: Regarded by society to be acceptable or good in character or quality.
vocabulary.example: A very respectable family.

vocabulary.definition: To make a continuous deep, resonant sound or to say something in a low heavy voice.
vocabulary.example: Uncle Lorenzo was heard to rumble that he could not understand.

vocabulary.definition: Narrow beams or columns of sunlight entering a space.
vocabulary.example: Dazzling gold in the shafts of sunlight from the windows.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that has a gently calming or relieving effect.
vocabulary.example: Rosa patted his arm soothingly.

vocabulary.definition: Better, greater, or different from what is usual.
vocabulary.example: Ours is a special family.

vocabulary.definition: An enlargement or increase in size; something becoming bigger.
vocabulary.example: Pointed green fruit was swelling where flowers had been.

vocabulary.definition: The natural ability or skill to perform magical acts or have magical powers.
vocabulary.example: Our family must marry someone with at least some talent for magic.

vocabulary.definition: Distressed or troubled emotionally.
vocabulary.example: He seemed so upset that Rosa comforted him.

vocabulary.definition: Extreme force or intensity, often of emotion or action.
vocabulary.example: He answered with a violence which surprised them.
